User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the NeuroQuest application, including their profile, gamified progress, and associated therapeutic data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and identification.

displayName.description: The public name chosen by the user for their profile.

level.description: The current therapeutic level of the user in the gamified system (e.g., Novato Mental, Explorador Mental).

currentXp.description: The current experience points accumulated by the user within their current level.

xpToNextLevel.description: The total experience points required for the user to advance to the next therapeutic level.

therapistIds.description: References to Therapists who are monitoring this user. (Relationship: User N:N Therapist)

isAdmin.description: Indicates if the user has administrative privileges for the platform (e.g., krakendigitalabs@gmail.com).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

Therapist.title: Therapist
Therapist.description: Represents a professional therapist using the NeuroQuest platform to monitor and support patients.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Therapist entity.

email.description: The therapist's email address, used for login and identification.

displayName.description: The name of the therapist displayed in the application.

specialization.description: The primary area of psychological specialization for the therapist (e.g., CBT, ERP for OCD).

patientIds.description: References to Users (patients) that this therapist is monitoring. (Relationship: Therapist N:N User)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the therapist account was created.

ThoughtRecord.title: ThoughtRecord
ThoughtRecord.description: Records an instance of a user identifying and logging an intrusive thought as part of the 'Observador Mental' module.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ThoughtRecord entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who recorded the thought. (Relationship: User 1:N ThoughtRecord)

recordedAt.description: Timestamp when the thought was recorded by the user.

thoughtText.description: The actual intrusive thought or mental event identified by the user.

cognitiveLabel.description: The cognitive label applied to the thought by the user (e.g., 'Pensamiento TOC', 'Catastrofización', 'Rumiación').

isFactNotThought.description: Indicates if the user identified the entry as a thought, not a fact.

associatedEmotion.description: The emotion associated with the intrusive thought (e.g., 'Ansiedad', 'Miedo', 'Tristeza').

intensity.description: The intensity of the thought/emotion on a scale (e.g., 1-10).

isIntrusive.description: Boolean indicating if the thought was identified as intrusive.

ExposureMission.title: ExposureMission
ExposureMission.description: Defines an Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) mission assigned to a user, a core element of the 'Modo Exposición'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExposureMission entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User for whom this mission is intended. (Relationship: User 1:N ExposureMission)

title.description: A concise title for the exposure mission (e.g., 'Tocar objeto sucio', 'No revisar la puerta').

description.description: A detailed description of the exposure mission and its objectives.

difficultyLevel.description: The difficulty level of the mission (e.g., 1 for easy, increasing progressively).

status.description: The current status of the mission (e.g., 'pending', 'active', 'completed', 'failed').

xpReward.description: The experience points awarded upon successful completion of the mission.

completionDate.description: Timestamp when the mission was successfully completed.

assignedBy.description: Indicates who or what assigned the mission (e.g., 'AI', 'Therapist', 'System').

therapistId.description: Optional reference to the Therapist who assigned this mission, if applicable. (Relationship: Therapist 1:N ExposureMission)

targetBehavior.description: The specific behavior the user needs to engage in or resist during the exposure (e.g., 'Touch a doorknob', 'Do not check the stove').

resistanceTarget.description: A specific quantifiable target for resistance or duration (e.g., 'not wash hands for 5 minutes', 'resist checking 1 time').

isAiGenerated.description: Indicates if this mission was dynamically generated by the AI based on user patterns.

ExerciseTemplate.title: ExerciseTemplate
ExerciseTemplate.description: Defines a reusable template for a general therapeutic exercise (e.g., breathing, grounding, cognitive reprogramming).

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExerciseTemplate entity.

description.description: A brief description of what the exercise entails.

instructions.description: Detailed step-by-step instructions for performing the exercise.

durationMinutes.description: The recommended duration of the exercise in minutes.

xpReward.description: The experience points awarded for completing this exercise.

UserExerciseLog.title: UserExerciseLog
UserExerciseLog.description: Tracks a specific instance of a user completing a general therapeutic exercise based on an ExerciseTemplate.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserExerciseLog entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who completed the exercise. (Relationship: User 1:N UserExerciseLog)

exerciseTemplateId.description: Reference to the ExerciseTemplate that was completed. (Relationship: ExerciseTemplate 1:N UserExerciseLog)

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the user completed the exercise.

xpEarned.description: The actual experience points earned by the user for this completion.

userNotes.description: Optional notes or reflections entered by the user after completing the exercise.

actualDurationMinutes.description: The actual duration in minutes the user spent on the exercise.

MentalCombatEvent.title: MentalCombatEvent
MentalCombatEvent.description: Records an instance of a user engaging in the gamified 'mental combat' against intrusive thoughts or compulsions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MentalCombatEvent entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who experienced the mental combat event. (Relationship: User 1:N MentalCombatEvent)

eventOccurredAt.description: Timestamp when the mental combat event occurred.

intruderName.description: The name of the 'intruder' or personification of the challenging thought (e.g., 'El Intruso', 'La Duda').

thoughtPresented.description: The specific intrusive thought or doubt presented during the combat event.

userAction.description: The action chosen by the user in response to the thought (e.g., 'Ignorar', 'Etiquetar como TOC', 'Respirar 60 segundos').

outcome.description: The result of the mental combat (e.g., 'Victory', 'Defeat', 'Neutral').

xpChange.description: The change in experience points resulting from this event (positive for victory, negative for defeat).

resistedCompulsion.description: Indicates if the user successfully resisted a compulsion during this event.

AnxietyLog.title: AnxietyLog
AnxietyLog.description: Allows users to log their anxiety levels, triggers, and coping strategies, contributing to overall progress tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AnxietyLog entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who logged the anxiety event. (Relationship: User 1:N AnxietyLog)

loggedAt.description: Timestamp when the anxiety level was logged.

anxietyLevel.description: The self-reported anxiety level on a numerical scale (e.g., 1-10).

triggerDescription.description: Optional description of what triggered the anxiety.

copingStrategyUsed.description: Optional description of the coping strategy employed (e.g., 'Breathing 4-6', 'Grounding 5-4-3-2-1', 'Ignored thought').

notes.description: Optional additional notes from the user about the anxiety event.

isCrisisIntervention.description: Indicates if this log entry is associated with the user accessing crisis resources or reporting high severity.

Achievement.title: Achievement
Achievement.description: Defines a gamified achievement, badge, or insignia that users can earn within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Achievement entity.

name.description: The name of the achievement (e.g., 'Primer acto de libertad', 'Medalla de autoconsciencia').

description.description: A detailed description of what the achievement represents.

criteria.description: A description of the conditions or actions required for a user to earn this achievement.

imageUrl.description: Optional URL to an image asset representing the achievement icon.

UserAchievement.title: UserAchievement
UserAchievement.description: Records an instance of a specific user having earned a particular achievement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserAchievement entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who earned the achievement. (Relationship: User 1:N UserAchievement)

achievementId.description: Reference to the Achievement definition that was earned. (Relationship: Achievement 1:N UserAchievement)

achievedAt.description: Timestamp when the achievement was earned by the user.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles, gamified progress, and includes a denormalized 'therapistIds' array to enable therapists to list their assigned patients. The 'isAdmin' field in the schema should be disregarded for global authorization, which is handled by the '/roles_admin/{adminId}' collection.

Stores therapist profiles, including 'patientIds' array to manage their assigned patients. This array is critical for securing therapist access to patient data and for synchronization with 'User.therapistIds'.

params.name: therapistId
params.description: The unique identifier of the therapist, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for Database Access Control (DBAC) to grant administrative privileges. The existence of a document with the user's UID as 'adminId' signifies administrator status. This adheres to 'Existence over Content' for global roles.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of the user who has administrative privileges.

definition.description: Stores individual intrusive thought records for a specific user. Ownership is path-based, ensuring user data privacy. Therapists can access these records if they are monitoring the 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this thought record.

params.name: recordId
params.description: The unique identifier for the thought record.

definition.description: Stores Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) missions assigned to a specific user. Ownership is path-based. Therapists can assign and monitor missions for patients they oversee.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to whom this exposure mission is assigned.

params.name: missionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exposure mission.

definition.description: Contains globally available templates for therapeutic exercises. This collection is read-only for all authenticated users and managed by administrators, ensuring a consistent and centralized source of exercises.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exercise template.

definition.description: Logs instances of a specific user completing a therapeutic exercise. This data is private to the user but viewable by their assigned therapists.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who completed the exercise.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exercise log entry.

definition.description: Records gamified mental combat events for a specific user, detailing interactions with intrusive thoughts. This data is private to the user but viewable by their assigned therapists.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who experienced the mental combat event.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for the mental combat event.

definition.description: Allows users to log their anxiety levels and triggers. This personal data is owned by the user but accessible to their assigned therapists for monitoring.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who logged the anxiety event.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for the anxiety log entry.

definition.description: Defines globally available achievements, badges, and their criteria. This collection is read-only for all authenticated users and managed by administrators.

params.name: achievementId
params.description: The unique identifier for the achievement.

definition.description: Records which achievements a specific user has earned. This data is owned by the user and is read-only for the user, with creation/updates handled by the system or administrators. Viewable by assigned therapists.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who earned the achievement.

params.name: userAchievementId
params.description: The unique identifier for this instance of a user earning an achievement.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for NeuroQuest prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access by adhering to the core design principles and mandates. The design leverages hierarchical paths for user-owned data, denormalization for authorization independence in specific scenarios, and structural segregation for clear security postures.

**Authorization Independence & QAPs:**

1.  **User-Owned Data:** All user-specific activity and progress data (`ThoughtRecord`, `ExposureMission`, `UserExerciseLog`, `MentalCombatEvent`, `AnxietyLog`, `UserAchievement`) is structured as subcollections under the user's profile path: `/users/{userId}/<subcollection>/{docId}`. This inherently provides strong path-based ownership, making security rules for users to access their *own* data extremely simple and efficient (`request.auth.uid == userId`). This directly supports QAPs for users, allowing them to list their own records without security filtering issues.

2.  **Therapist Access to Patient Data (N:N Relationship):**
    *   **Therapist Profile (`/therapists/{therapistId}`):** Each therapist document contains a `patientIds` array, which lists the `userId`s of all patients they monitor. This acts as the primary source of truth for a therapist's patient roster.
    *   **User Profile (`/users/{userId}`):** The `User` document includes a denormalized `therapistIds` array. This denormalization is crucial for two reasons:
        *   **Therapist QAPs for Listing Patients:** It enables therapists to efficiently query and list all their assigned patients (`/users`) by filtering on `therapistIds` containing their own UID. This satisfies the QAP requirement for listing. For example, `db.collection('users').where('therapistIds', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid).get()` is a secure and performant query.
        *   **User Visibility:** Allows users to easily see which therapists are monitoring them.
    *   **Access to Patient Subcollections:** To grant therapists access to a *specific patient's subcollection data* (e.g., a patient's `ThoughtRecord`), the security rules will perform a `get()` operation on the *therapist's own document* (`/therapists/$(request.auth.uid)`). This allows the rule to check if the `patientId` (from the data path) is present in the therapist's `patientIds` array. This is *not* a hierarchical dependency in the sense of a parent-child relationship within the patient's data, but rather a cross-collection lookup for the *accessor's* permissions. This approach respects the
